texto: Much of the genre pretty much sounds like popular acoustic songs you'd hear on mainstream radio. The only difference here is that the front man, instead of singing "I love her," switches the words to "I love him," referring to Jesus. Which ends up sounding just a tad gay. That irony is apparently lost on this spiritual set.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Benyamin Cohen: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “Much of the genre pretty much sounds like popular acoustic songs you'd hear on mainstream radio. …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que fe, christian-living, christian-music pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e christian-music.
